Eleanor Finch

Award-winning novelist

12

Published novels

Author of the month

Meet Eleanor Finch

Eleanor Finch writes luminous, character-driven fiction that lingers long after the final page. A two-time national book award finalist, her work explores memory, belonging, and the quiet courage of ordinary lives.

"I write for the reader who finishes a book and sits quietly for a moment, changed."

Featured works

The Lantern of Aldridge Bay

Salt & Starlight

Where the Rivers Remember

Explore all her books

The art of the slow read

May 28, 2026·7 min read
Why reading slowly is the ultimate luxury

In a world of endless scrolling, savouring a single page might be the most radical thing you do all week.

Read article

The Lantern of Aldridge Bay

Fiction

The Lantern of Aldridge Bay

by Eleanor Finch
4.8 (2,341 reviews)

A luminous debut about a lighthouse keeper's daughter who uncovers a century-old secret buried beneath the tides. A sweeping story of memory, loss, and the light we leave behind.

$16.99
Full details
